description: Process supervision significantly outperforms outcome supervision, kickstarting the reasoning model paradigm and test-time compute scaling.

evidence_links.label: Lightman, H., et al. (2023). Let's Verify Step by Step. arXiv.
evidence_links.note: Demonstrates 78% solve rate on MATH dataset. Highly cited by capabilities papers like Deepseek-R1.

description: Replacing human feedback with AI-generated feedback. Framed as safety research but RLAIF was adopted for capability training.

evidence_links.label: Bai, Y., et al. (2022). Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback. arXiv.
evidence_links.note: The paper contends that its constitutional approach may be reframed into guidelines for optimizing AI capabilities.

description: Retrospective analysis of RLHF's transition from an alignment technique to a core driver of general model performance.

evidence_links.label: Ziegler, D. M., et al. (2019). Fine-Tuning Language Models from Human Preferences. arXiv.
evidence_links.note: Authors directly contend that advances to scalable reward learning occur in tandem with capability advancement.

description: Foundational mechanistic interpretability work introducing the 'circuits' framework for understanding neural network internals.

evidence_links.label: Olah, C., et al. (2020). Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits. Distill.
evidence_links.note: Introduces tools highly likely to kick off subfields with uses in capability areas across training and deployment.
